FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Trump's CFPB pick gains workforce discretion, Fed rate-hike odds climb on geopolitical tensions, and big banks set up for strong Q2 earnings.

bullets.text: CFPB nominee Brian Johnson wins 60-day post-confirmation review period on staff cuts, giving Trump's pick direct control over bureau workforce reduction plans.
July Fed rate-hike odds rising on Strait of Hormuz oil-price spike—direct pressure on bank NIM compression and deposit-pricing strategies heading into earnings season.
Q2 bank earnings expected strong on SpaceX IPO advisory fees, geopolitical volatility trading gains, and uptick in commercial lending activity.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments goes non-profit under CalPERS, RIA consolidation accelerates across platforms, and a fintech regulatory hiccup surfaces.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments for $580B pension system; Russell sheds debt and pivots to non-profit governance following the Vanguard playbook for reputational lift.
RIA consolidation hits overdrive: Hightower's organic growth soars, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, Choreo closes fourth acquisition post-SpaceX, Wealthfront crosses $100B AUM.
Summitry founder Colin Higgins steps to executive chairman with two co-CEOs elevated; $4B Aspen-owned RIA undergoes leadership refresh after 22-year tenure.
Betterment hit with class action lawsuit over cash sweep accounts allegedly funneling client deposits into non-interest-bearing accounts without compensation.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: KKR-led consortium bids $5.3B for Aussie broker amid strong US P&C underwriting cycle; geopolitical risks flare in Strait of Hormuz.

bullets.text: KKR joins Amwins and Dragoneer in A$7.7B ($5.33B) bid for Steadfast, Australia's largest insurance broker—consolidation heating up in the space.
US P&C industry posts strongest underwriting profit in a decade with $84B in two-year gains and lowest combined ratio in 10 years; but performance is bifurcated across lines.
Iran-US missile exchange near Strait of Hormuz drives oil spike, threatening energy, shipping, and marine coverage with geopolitical tail risk.
Canada's banking regulator warns institutions on cyber risks from Anthropic's Claude AI—compressed response windows and expanded threat surface pose emerging underwriting questions.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Mastercard explores Vocalink exit while CMBS distress accelerates; mixed signals on fintech expansion and private debt demand.

bullets.text: Mastercard shopping Vocalink sale—potential return of UK payments platform to original banking consortium after decade-long hold. Strategic reassessment of non-core assets.
CMBS special servicing rates spike in June 2026 as retail, office, and lodging assets deteriorate—signals mounting servicer workouts and credit quality erosion across property securitizations.
North America private debt still LP darling despite market divergence between performing/distressed credits, per AlbaCore; capital remains available but selectivity rising.
Klarna faces Dutch class action lawsuit while Nubank clears Mexican banking authorization—fintech regulatory headwinds in Europe offset by LatAm expansion tailwinds.

PE & Deals.lede: Arctos deploys novel GP financing structure while secondaries market heats up on both LP-led and institutional capital fronts.

bullets.text: Arctos Partners closes $6.2B GP financing using 'self-liquidating' Keystone structure—a first-of-its-kind deployment that lets the fund return capital to LPs while maintaining portfolio exposure.
Moelis poaches four-person LP-led secondaries team from Evercore, signaling major expansion into the fastest-growing segment of the secondary markets.
LP-led secondaries market accelerating with LGT, Dawson, and Pomona actively sourcing as favorable pricing and liquidity conditions drive seller appetite.
Korean pension KBIZ commits ~$230M to domestic blind-pool PE/VC funds for 2026, signaling institutional capital rotation toward Korea-focused managers.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings season opens amid QE legacy risks, while small-cap rotation and central bank pilots reshape market structure.

bullets.text: Bank earnings kick off Tuesday with elevated expectations and disappointment risk looming—major lenders face high bar after recent repricing.
QE fundamentally rewired bank funding models to depend on central bank liquidity; QT poses greater systemic risk than policymakers acknowledge, warns FT analyst.
Russell 2000 on best year since 2003 as capital rotation away from mega-cap tech drives small-cap rally—valuation relief and tax policy tailwinds fueling the move.
UK gilt yields breach 5% as oil surges, signaling fiscal sustainability concerns just as new PM arrives; ECB simultaneously recruits 36 banks including Deutsche for 2025 digital euro stress-test.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings dominate tomorrow; ETF flows turn negative; and policy shifts are reshaping advisory workflows across wealth management.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—credit quality, deposit dynamics, and consumer health will be under the microscope across lending, trading, and wealth management.
Rare week of negative ETF flows signals broader market sentiment shift; redemptions hitting equity, bond, and alternative products.
Vanguard and Envestnet partner to reduce tax friction in model portfolio conversions—addresses key RIA workflow pain point as advisors manage client transitions.
Student loan policy overhaul forcing financial advisors to rebuild college planning strategies, reshaping demand for education financing and advisory services.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: AI moats are shifting from model capability to defensible architecture; startup financing gets creative; and incentive misalignment dogs even elite performance.

bullets.text: Open-source AI now matches frontier models at 1/10th cost, forcing founders to compete on moat type, not model quality—a fundamental industry reset.
Groq's Jonathan Ross closed a $20B NVIDIA deal via unconventional financing (Grok Bonds) after near-bankruptcy—illustrates how creative capital solutions matter when traditional funding stalls.
NBA players gaming shooting stats by taking end-of-quarter heaves shows how broken performance metrics create principal-agent problems that destroy real value.

all.lede: Big week for bank earnings amid QE/QT debate; KKR consortium bids $5.3B for Australian broker; Mastercard explores Vocalink exit.

bullets.text: KKR, Amwins, and Dragoneer submitted A$7.7B ($5.3B) bid for Steadfast Group, Australia's largest insurance broker, in a competitive process.
Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—critical read on credit quality, deposit health, and consumer spending across the portfolio.
Mastercard weighs sale of Vocalink (UK payments platform acquired 2016) potentially back to original banking consortium owners.
QE structurally changed bank balance sheets and funding models, making QT riskier than policymakers assume due to banks' reliance on central bank liquidity.
